name: The Book of Mormon
description: The 1830 LDS scripture and the 2011 Broadway musical that satirized it.

questions.q: In what year was the Book of Mormon first published?

source.notes: Published in Palmyra, New York.

questions.q: Who is the religious figure credited with translating and publishing the Book of Mormon?

source.notes: Founder of the Latter Day Saint movement.

questions.q: According to Joseph Smith, which angel appeared to him and revealed the location of the golden plates?

source.notes: Statues of Moroni stand atop most LDS temples.

questions.q: On what New York hillside did Joseph Smith say he found the golden plates?

source.notes: Located near Palmyra, NY. Pageants used to be held there annually.

questions.q: What language did Joseph Smith say the golden plates were written in?

source.notes: An otherwise-unattested script per Mormon scripture.

questions.q: How many witnesses signed statements affirming they saw the golden plates?

source.notes: Three Witnesses + Eight Witnesses (and Joseph Smith).

questions.q: Which prophet, after whom the book is named, is said to have compiled and edited most of its records?

source.notes: His son Moroni completed the record after Mormon's death.

questions.q: According to the Book of Mormon, which family fled Jerusalem around 600 BCE for the Americas?

source.notes: Lehi's family included his sons Nephi, Laman, and Lemuel.

questions.q: What are the names of the two warring civilizations in the Book of Mormon's narrative?

source.notes: Descendants of Nephi vs. descendants of Laman, both tracing back to Lehi.

questions.q: In which book of the Book of Mormon does the resurrected Jesus reportedly visit the Americas?

source.notes: Often considered the climax of the narrative.

questions.q: What is the name of the Mormon Church's Salt Lake City-based world headquarters denomination?
choices: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

source.notes: Often abbreviated 'LDS Church' or just 'the Church.' Members called Latter-day Saints (LDS) or Mormons.

questions.q: Which leader led the Mormon pioneers westward to the Salt Lake Valley after Joseph Smith's death?

source.notes: Arrived in 1847; founded Salt Lake City.

questions.q: Who is the chief LDS leader, the equivalent of pope in Mormonism?

source.notes: Considered the prophet, seer, and revelator.

questions.q: What is the name of the LDS-affiliated youth missionary program in which young members typically serve a 1.5–2 year mission?

source.notes: Members typically serve a full-time mission, often abroad. Famously the inspiration for the Broadway musical.

questions.q: Which 2011 Broadway musical satirizes Mormon missionaries in Uganda?

source.notes: By Trey Parker, Matt Stone, and Robert Lopez.

questions.q: Which 'South Park' co-creators wrote the music and book for the Broadway musical 'The Book of Mormon'?
choices: Matt Groening and David X. Cohen

source.notes: Co-written with Robert Lopez (Avenue Q, Frozen).

questions.q: How many Tony Awards did 'The Book of Mormon' musical win at the 2011 Tonys?

source.notes: Including Best Musical.

questions.q: What U.S. state is the headquarters of the LDS Church and home to its main temple?

source.notes: Salt Lake City. Salt Lake Temple opened 1893.

questions.q: Which famous American family drama TV show, created by Big Love writer Mark V. Olsen, depicts a fundamentalist Mormon polygamist family?

source.notes: Aired on HBO 2006-2011, starring Bill Paxton.

questions.q: What is the title of the catchy opening number of the Broadway musical 'The Book of Mormon'?

source.notes: Sung by missionaries practicing their door-knocking.
